name: Prairies de la Mer - Port Grimaud - Sainte-Maxime
surface: Coastal cycle path / shared local roads; exact surface segments to verify
start: Prairies de la Mer / Port Grimaud
water_points: Port Grimaud and Sainte-Maxime public fountains/cafes to verify
traffic_notes: Seed GPX uses control points only; summer traffic and crossings around Port Grimaud and Sainte-Maxime need local review.

name: Port Grimaud - Sainte-Maxime - Fréjus V65 seed
surface: Parcours Cyclable du Littoral / V65 segments and shared-road links to verify
water_points: Sainte-Maxime and beach towns to verify
traffic_notes: Use as a research track only; exact V65 geometry and any discontinuities must be checked against official route data.

name: Gassin - Ramatuelle - Pampelonne road loop
surface: Road loop; gradients, traffic and shoulders to verify
coffee_stops: Gassin, Ramatuelle village, Pampelonne/Saint-Tropez stops to verify
water_points: Villages and beach sectors to verify
traffic_notes: Seed GPX favors scenic controls; avoid peak summer traffic until local road safety is checked.

name: Collebasse loop: Cogolin - Gassin - Ramatuelle - Col de Collebasse - La Croix-Valmer
surface: Paved throughout; Col de Collebasse 3.1 km at 3.1% (max ~7%), summit 130 m
traffic_notes: Ride before 9am in summer; D93/D61 busy from mid-morning; descent to La Croix-Valmer has good sightlines.

name: Corniche des Maures: Cogolin - La Môle - Col du Canadel - Rayol - Cavalaire loop
surface: Paved; Col du Canadel summit 267 m, south side balcony road over the sea
traffic_notes: D98 through La Môle valley carries traffic; corniche narrow in places; best early morning outside July-August weekends.

name: Boucle des Maures: Grimaud - Canadel - Babaou - Collobrières - Taillude - Grimaud
surface: Paved mountain back roads; Col du Babaou 414 m; Col de Taillude 8 km at 3.25% from Collobrières
traffic_notes: Long remote sections without services; check heat and water plan; quiet traffic inland year-round.

name: Peninsula tour: Saint-Tropez - Les Salins - Pampelonne - l'Escalet - La Croix-Valmer - Gassin
surface: Paved; rolling capes roads, short punchy climbs around Escalet
traffic_notes: Beach traffic on D93 from 10am in season; route safest clockwise early morning.

name: Piste des Crêtes: Grimaud - Maures ridge - La Garde-Freinet
surface: Forest gravel tracks along the Maures ridge line; loose surfaces on descents
water_points: La Garde-Freinet fountain; no water on the ridge
traffic_notes: Massif access closed on high fire-risk summer days - check Var massif-access map; signature ride of local Maures Gravel riders.

name: Plaine des Maures and Lac des Escarcets loop
surface: Sandy and rocky reserve tracks; stay on authorised routes
traffic_notes: Nature reserve rules apply; summer closures possible; best in spring and autumn.

name: Maures traverse towards Notre-Dame des Anges
surface: Long forest pistes climbing to 767 m at Notre-Dame des Anges
water_points: No reliable water above the villages
traffic_notes: Remote; navigation and fire-risk closures must be checked; for experienced gravel riders only.

name: Family ride: Saint-Tropez - Les Salins - Pampelonne north
surface: Mixed quiet lanes and short D-road links; mostly gentle
coffee_stops: Les Salins beach kiosks, Pampelonne north
traffic_notes: Quiet outside season; in July-August ride before 10am; some narrow lane sections shared with cars.

name: E-bike village ride: Port Grimaud - Cogolin - Grimaud village
surface: Paved; short climb to Grimaud village
traffic_notes: Climb to Grimaud has moderate traffic; secure parking at village entrance; market mornings busiest.
